channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saleem Rashid: From Orphaned Teen to Viral Home Chef in Durban
item.description: Saleem Rashid, a 52-year-old from Clare Estate in Durban, began cooking out of necessity after being orphaned as a young boy and living in an orphanage. His passion for cooking grew while living with ...

item.title: Goldman and Lander clash over Israel policy in competitive NYC congressional primary
item.description: A heated debate over Israel has become a central dividing line in the Democratic primary between U.S. Rep. Dan Goldman and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander in a closely watched Manhattan-B...

item.title: Matthias Forster’s Photographic Exploration of Human Control and Chance in Everyday Landscapes
item.description: This LensCulture feature presents the work of Swiss photographer Matthias Forster and his series titled “Homo Faber and Divine Providence,” which explores the relationship between human control and th...

item.title: Regional parties support OPAPRU neutrality ahead of BARMM parliamentary elections
item.description: Regional political parties in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) have expressed support and appreciation for the Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation an...

item.title: Key developments on May 28: DOJ probe into E. Jean Carroll, renewed Iran–US strikes, Trump health update, redistricting shifts, and Laos cave rescue effort
item.description: This briefing outlines five major news developments for May 28 covering legal, geopolitical, political, and humanitarian stories. First, the U.S. Department of Justice has opened a criminal investigat...

item.title: ANU staff argue Julie Bishop’s resignation reflects governance dynamics rather than loss of university autonomy
item.description: This opinion piece, written by staff members of the Australian National University (ANU), responds to public commentary surrounding the resignation of former Chancellor Julie Bishop on 8 May 2026. The...

item.title: Newfoundland and Labrador to track criminal cases dismissed due to trial delays
item.description: Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Tony Wakeham has declared it 'clearly unacceptable' that criminal cases in the province are being dismissed due to delays in the court system. This comes after a CBC ...

item.title: White House Staff Directed to Focus on Messaging as Trump Pursues Personal Projects Amid Falling Approval
item.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles has reportedly instructed aides to follow a 'let Trump be Trump' approach, allowing President Donald Trump to focus on personal projects rather than addressing p...

item.title: Jackson Chauke opts for training over birthday celebrations ahead of July fight with Gideon Buthelezi
item.description: Veteran South African boxer Jackson “M3” Chauke will not be celebrating his 41st birthday in the usual way, as he instead focuses on training for his upcoming fight in July against former three-weight...

item.title: Hawthorn dominate early as St Kilda struggle with injuries in AFL Round 12 clash at Marvel Stadium
item.description: Hawthorn have taken control of their AFL Round 12 clash against St Kilda at Marvel Stadium, producing a dominant first-half display that left the Saints reeling. At half-time, the Hawks led 9.7 (61) t...

item.title: IMD issues orange alert for Delhi-NCR as thunderstorms and hail expected; red alert in Gurugram
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a fresh weather warning for Delhi-NCR, forecasting a significant shift in weather conditions after an intense heatwave. According to the advisory, ...

item.title: Carney to promote Canada as investment destination during New York business visit
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is visiting New York City to meet with business leaders and present Canada as a prime destination for international investment. During the trip, he is expected to hold priva...

item.title: Maura Higgins promotes chicken wings in glamorous campaign amid growing US television and film career
item.description: Maura Higgins, the former Love Island contestant, has appeared in a promotional campaign for a chicken wing brand while continuing to build her profile in the United States entertainment industry. In ...

item.title: Report alleges Treasury officials pushed proposal to place Donald Trump’s image on U.S. currency despite legal restrictions
item.description: An investigative report alleges that two political appointees in the U.S. Treasury Department pressured staff at the Bureau of Engraving and Printing to advance a proposal that would place former Pres...

item.title: Man convicted of murder after fatal stabbing of Southampton student
item.description: A 23-year-old man, Vickrum Digwa, has been found guilty at Southampton Crown Court of murdering 18-year-old university student Henry Nowak in a fatal stabbing incident that took place on 3 December. T...

item.title: A calm, storage-focused redesign transforms a Riverdale family home in Toronto
item.description: A Toronto-based interior redesign project in the city’s east end reimagines a compact family home into a calm, functional, and visually cohesive living space. The homeowners, a young family with a dog...

item.title: Major fire destroys shops worth millions at Katsina Central Market amid response delays
item.description: One serious fire outbreak for Katsina Central Market early Wednesday morning don destroy many shops and goods worth hundreds of millions of naira. According to eyewitnesses, the fire start before dayb...

item.title: Ruto clarifies his meeting with matatu leaders on fuel crisis and strike cancellation decision
item.description: President William Ruto has clarified that he did not instruct transport sector stakeholders, including matatu leaders, to call off their planned strike. Speaking during the National Prayer Breakfast h...

item.title: Edmonton Elks rebuild receiving corps with new talent and veteran leadership ahead of 2026 season
item.description: Edmonton Elks quarterback Cody Fajardo is expressing optimism about the team’s retooled receiving corps heading into the 2026 CFL season. After an off-season focused on improving size, versatility, an...

item.title: Enhanced Games Faces Investor Setbacks as Event Fails to Impress
item.description: The Enhanced Games, a sporting event where athletes are permitted to use performance-enhancing substances, suffered a disappointing weekend both in competition and financially. The event failed to del...
